title: Keep a one-command rollback to the last good model
description: Maintain the previously serving model version warm and a single switch to restore it when live metrics regress after a rollout.
body: Use when cutting production traffic over to a newly promoted model. Do not delete or unload the prior version on cutover — if the new model regresses you need an instant restore, not a rebuild. Actions: keep the last-good version registered and loadable, wire a single rollback switch, and run it as a drill before relying on it. Validation signals include a rehearsed rollback that restores the prior version inside the SLO and a serving config that records which version is live. Risk boundary: a stuck-forward rollout with no escape harms users every request; if rollback is untested, treat the deployment as blocked and escalate.
